title: Python: visualize.py topology_from_states returns str for parent but DodagState uses IPv6Address
description:
In python/src/lichen/rpl/visualize.py:24-28, topology_from_states() was updated to call str(state.preferred_parent) because DodagState.preferred_parent is now IPv6Address.

However, this creates an inconsistency: the Topology type alias is dict[str, str | None], but other parts of the visualization code may expect the parent values to be the same type as the node_id keys. The test at test_visualize.py:49 now compares topo['child'] to str(root_addr) rather than the original pattern.

Location: python/src/lichen/rpl/visualize.py:24-28
Risk: Downstream code using Topology may break if it expects consistent types.
Fix: Either update Topology type alias to show this asymmetry, or normalize all identifiers to the same type.
